text: Synthetic Studies towards Quinazoline Derivatives (Mini-Review) Quinazoline derivatives as a consequence of their rich and varied biological activities are among the molecules studied extensively in present day synthetic chemistry and pharmacology. The present article is a critical survey of the progress made in this direction in the recent years. The different methods employed for the construction of quinazoline derivatives such as aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal mediated processes, ultrasound assisted reactions and phase-transfer catalysis are grouped under five heads and illustrated with several important studies. The potential biological activities ascertained through studies of newly synthesized molecules have also been discussed alongwith their possible application in drug design.
explanation: Polished, generic abstract language with no distinctive voice; the phrasing and structure are typical of AI-generated summaries as well as many human abstracts, giving a moderate likelihood.
